LHX.sentence: L3Harris ticked up a little and sits well below its high for the year, but defense spending keeps rolling along, so the business itself hasn't really changed.

ASML.sentence: ASML had a good week and keeps doing what it does — they're the only company in the world making a certain kind of chipmaking machine, so demand just keeps coming.

LIN.sentence: Linde is basically flat — industrial gas demand is steady, though the very strong US dollar will make their overseas sales look a bit smaller when they next report.

MSFT.sentence: No fresh data on Microsoft today, so nothing to report.

TMO.sentence: No data came through on Thermo Fisher today, so there's nothing new to report.

ENB.sentence: Enbridge is steady, but its dividend payout is barely better than what you'd get from safe government bonds right now, so it's looking a touch expensive for what it offers.

NOW.sentence: No data on ServiceNow today, so nothing to mention.

GLNCY.sentence: Glencore edged up, and while its mining profits are at a low point right now, that kind of trough has historically been the bottom of the cycle for a company like this.

IBIT.sentence: Bitcoin is sitting well below its recent average and momentum is weak, so even though it ticked up the bargain-hunting signal here has a poor track record.

KOF.sentence: The Latin American Coke bottler had a quiet up day, with the Mexican peso holding steady — which matters because most of its sales are in Mexico.

PBR.A.sentence: Petrobras has dropped sharply even though oil prices are high, which suggests the selloff is about Brazilian politics and dividend worries rather than the oil business itself.

AMKBY.sentence: Maersk, the big Danish container-shipping company, slipped a bit and is now valued at less than what its fleet of ships would cost to replace — a sign the market is feeling cautious about global trade.
